work.name: University of California, San Diego
highlights: Designing a domain-adaptation framework to mitigate bias in AI systems.

Led a mixed-methods study training nine paired conditional GANs to replicate Instagram beauty-filter transformations, and directed a student team building a Smart Mirror system (Java/HTML/CSS, Python/Flask, SQL + Supabase) to deploy the models live, run user surveys, and audit how filter-induced changes shift AI predictions of race, age, gender, and emotion.

Built a bias-auditing pipeline using computer vision, Python, pretrained models, and statistical modeling to quantify how beauty filters alter facial features, and what role Western beauty standards play in that shift.

work.name: University of California, San Diego
highlights: Held weekly office hours and graded coursework on core architectures (MLPs, CNNs, RNNs) and foundational methods (backpropagation, automatic differentiation).
Designed homework on recurrent neural networks and lectured on CNN architecture for undergraduate deep learning instruction.

work.name: Xerox-Lexmark
highlights: Built an agentic automation prototype in Databricks (PySpark, SQL, Python, LangChain) to streamline data cleaning across the Medallion architecture.

Fine-tuned Microsoft's Florence VLM on Azure ML to classify and diagnose print-quality errors; improved inference through prompt engineering and tracked experiments with MLflow and Optuna/manual hyperparameter tuning.
Researched agentic automation with Claude and MCP servers, designing prompt-driven pipelines for data cleaning and ingestion.
Joined cross-functional reviews to assess model-pipeline production readiness.

work.name: University of California, San Diego
work.position: Teaching Assistant: Machine Learning for Music
highlights: Built and maintained course notebooks applying logistic regression, SVMs, k-NN, TF-IDF, and stratified k-fold cross-validation.
Wrote technical slide content on linear regression, neural networks, activation functions, CNNs, and evaluation metrics.

work.name: University of California, San Diego
highlights: Supported student learning on prototyping, social computing, and interface evaluation.
Reviewed student-led discussions and paper commentaries to sharpen critical analysis.

work.name: Xerox-Lexmark
highlights: Transformed raw data into customer-preferred formats and built a cleansing pipeline using Databricks' Medallion architecture, Python, and SQL.
Helped migrate data from the Big Decisions platform into Databricks.

work.name: Xavier University of Louisiana, Computer Science Department
highlights: Used the Flickr Faces dataset to study facial-recognition accuracy gaps between lighter and darker skin tones, and logged false positives in the Haar Cascade algorithm.
Tested edge detection for textured/coily hair, comparing afro and straight hair via the Structural Similarity Index.

work.name: University of California, San Diego STARS Research Program
highlights: Helped build a framework to precisely locate a drone via an attached microchip, including a Python script to identify the chip's position.
Used a Kalman filter to fuse IMU and microchip data for a refined position estimate.

volunteer.summary: Led a Capture the Flag workshop teaching cyber-safety to high schoolers from underrepresented backgrounds through interactive challenges.
Organized a small-scale Capture the Flag workshop teaching online safety to middle schoolers from underrepresented backgrounds.
Mentored undergraduate HBCU students on Ph.D. preparation through weekly meetings covering imposter syndrome, conference presentation, abstract writing, and reading/writing research papers.

courses: Theoretical Aspects of Large Language Models
